meta_title: Roofing in Lauderdale Lakes, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Expert roofing in Lauderdale Lakes, FL. CertainTeed & West Lake hurricane-rated systems. Licensed, local, A+ BBB. Free estimates — financing from $87/mo.
h1: Trusted Roofing Replacement & Repair in Lauderdale Lakes, FL
intro:
Lauderdale Lakes is a tight-knit Broward County community where homeownership is a point of real pride. From the quiet, tree-lined streets of Oakland Forest to the lakeside properties surrounding Lake Emerald and the residential blocks near Vincent Torres Memorial Park, local homeowners have invested deeply in their properties — and that investment starts with the roof over their heads. With average home values hovering around $250,000, a compromised roof isn't just a safety concern; it's a direct threat to your most significant financial asset. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is here to make sure that asset is protected for decades to come.

What makes roofing in Lauderdale Lakes particularly pressing is the age of the local housing stock. Approximately 60% of homes in this area were built before 1994 — which means they predate the modern Florida Building Code overhaul that followed Hurricane Andrew. Those older roofs were installed to standards that today's storm environment has long since outpaced. The city sits about 6 miles from the Atlantic coastline, placing it squarely in a 170 mph wind zone. While Lauderdale Lakes is not in the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), the Florida Building Code still mandates hurricane-rated roofing products and installation methods that can withstand the intense wind forces Broward County regularly experiences during storm season.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has completed more than 500 homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, and our crews are deeply familiar with the specific roofing challenges that Lauderdale Lakes homeowners face — from aging underlayment systems to outdated fastening patterns and worn-out shingle surfaces that no longer provide adequate weather resistance. Whether you're dealing with storm damage, chronic leaking, or simply a roof that has reached the end of its service life, our team brings the expertise, the right products, and a genuine commitment to quality workmanship that has earned us an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau and the reputation as the Kings of Service throughout South Florida.

section_why.h2: Why Lauderdale Lakes Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Roof Replacement
section_why.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we don't subcontract our work — ever. Every roof we install in Lauderdale Lakes is handled entirely by our own trained, licensed, and insured crews. This matters more than many homeowners realize. When a roofing company subs out its labor, the quality of workmanship can vary wildly from job to job, and accountability becomes murky. With Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, the same experienced team that shows up to assess your roof is the team that installs it, inspects it, and stands behind it. Our crews bring more than 60 years of combined experience in South Florida roofing, and they understand the regional nuances that generic national contractors simply don't.

We're also deeply familiar with the permit process through the City of Lauderdale Lakes Building Division. Pulling proper permits isn't just a legal requirement — it's your protection. An unpermitted roof can void your homeowner's insurance coverage, create serious complications when you go to sell your home, and leave you exposed to liability if a future storm causes damage. Our team handles the permitting process from start to finish, ensuring every installation meets or exceeds the requirements set by both the City of Lauderdale Lakes and Broward County.

Our product selection is purpose-built for South Florida's climate. We install CertainTeed architectural shingles from the Landmark series and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems — both engineered specifically for high-wind environments and designed to perform reliably in the heat, humidity, and storm exposure that define life in Broward County. These are not entry-level products; they are premium, tested systems backed by strong manufacturer warranties and a proven track record in the field.

Perhaps most importantly, we treat every Lauderdale Lakes homeowner the way we'd want a contractor to treat our own family. We provide transparent, itemized estimates with no hidden fees, we communicate clearly throughout the project, and we don't consider a job complete until you're fully satisfied. That commitment to service is what the Kings of Service name truly means.

section_history.h2: Lessons from the Storms: Why Lauderdale Lakes Roofs Face Constant Pressure
section_history.body:
Lauderdale Lakes has been tested by no fewer than 9 significant named storms since 1990, and the damage patterns those storms left behind tell a clear story about what roofing systems in this community need to withstand. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was the wake-up call that reshaped Florida's entire approach to building codes, but many homes in Lauderdale Lakes were already built by then — and many were not upgraded in the years that followed. When Hurricane Wilma tore through Broward County in 2005, the widespread roof damage it caused was concentrated heavily in older neighborhoods with pre-code construction, exactly the type of housing stock that makes up a large portion of Lauderdale Lakes. Wilma's broad circulation and extended wind event stressed roofing systems in ways that punctuated, intense storms like Andrew sometimes don't — lifting shingles, breaking tile systems, and compromising flashing installations that simply weren't designed for sustained high-wind exposure.

Hurricane Irma in 2017 reinforced those lessons. Though Irma made landfall on Florida's southwest coast, its reach extended well into Broward County, and homes throughout the area experienced significant roof damage — particularly those with aging materials or marginal installation quality. The neighborhoods around Lake Emerald and Oakland Forest saw their share of blown-off shingles, cracked tiles, and damaged fascia boards during Irma's passage. For homeowners who had deferred roof replacement, Irma was often the tipping point.

The cumulative effect of nearly three decades of storm exposure on Lauderdale Lakes roofs cannot be overstated. Even roofs that weren't visibly devastated by any single hurricane have absorbed years of wind uplift stress, UV degradation, and moisture intrusion events that gradually erode their performance. A roof that looks acceptable today may be dangerously close to failure when the next major storm arrives. The time to address roofing vulnerabilities in Lauderdale Lakes is before hurricane season — not during it.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Roofing Requirements for Lauderdale Lakes Homeowners
section_code.body:
Although Lauderdale Lakes falls outside the High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) designation — which applies to Miami-Dade and Broward coastal communities — this does not mean roofing standards here are relaxed. The Florida Building Code still mandates that all roofing products used in this wind zone carry ratings sufficient to withstand 170 mph wind speeds. That means every shingle, tile, fastener, underlayment, and adhesive system used on a Lauderdale Lakes roof must be tested and approved for high-wind performance. Products that do not meet these standards cannot legally be installed, and any contractor willing to cut those corners is putting your home — and your insurance coverage — at serious risk.

The Florida Building Code also requires specific installation protocols that go well beyond simply laying new materials over old ones. In most cases, full tear-off of the existing roofing system is required to inspect the deck, replace damaged sheathing, and ensure proper adhesion of the new system. Underlayment requirements are strict, and the fastening pattern for shingles and tiles must meet prescribed pull-out resistance values. These aren't bureaucratic formalities — they are engineering requirements developed specifically in response to the damage patterns observed in hurricanes like Andrew, Wilma, and Irma.

All roofing work in Lauderdale Lakes requires permits issued through the City of Lauderdale Lakes Building Division, followed by inspections to verify compliance at key stages of installation. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages this entire process for our customers. We prepare the permit application, coordinate inspection scheduling, and ensure our crews are executing every phase of the installation in strict accordance with code. Homeowners who work with unlicensed contractors or permit-free installers risk having to tear out and redo the entire roof at their own expense — a scenario we've helped many Lauderdale Lakes families avoid by doing things right the first time.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Landmark Shingles & West Lake Tile: Premium Roofing Built for Broward County
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two premium roofing systems in Lauderdale Lakes, each selected for its proven performance in South Florida's demanding climate: CertainTeed architectural shingles from the Landmark series and West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. These are not warehouse-grade products — they are engineered roofing solutions with tested wind resistance ratings, robust warranty programs, and long track records in high-wind coastal environments.

CertainTeed's Landmark series is one of the most recognized architectural shingle lines in the industry, and for good reason. These dimensional shingles feature a dual-layer construction that creates depth, shadow, and visual character while delivering Class A fire resistance and wind resistance ratings that meet Florida Building Code requirements for our wind zone. The Landmark shingles are algae-resistant — an important feature in Broward County's humid subtropical climate, where roof algae and discoloration are common complaints among homeowners with lesser products. CertainTeed backs the Landmark series with a strong limited lifetime warranty, and their SureStart protection provides full replacement coverage in the early years of ownership. For Lauderdale Lakes homeowners looking for a cost-effective, high-performing shingle upgrade, the Landmark series is a flagship choice.

For homeowners who prefer the classic Florida aesthetic of tile roofing, West Lake concrete and clay tile systems offer an exceptional combination of durability, beauty, and storm resistance. Tile roofs are a proven performer in high-wind environments — when properly installed with hurricane-rated fastening systems and appropriate underlayment, they can outlast shingle roofs by decades. West Lake's product line is manufactured to meet the strict testing requirements of Florida's building codes, and their tile systems are available in a wide range of profiles and colors to complement any Lauderdale Lakes home's architectural character. Whether you're in Oakland Forest or near the C&I Studios corridor, a West Lake tile roof adds curb appeal and long-term value that is difficult to match.

section_cost.h2: Roofing Costs and Financing Options for Lauderdale Lakes Homeowners
section_cost.body:
For homeowners in Lauderdale Lakes — where average home values sit around $250,000 — a full roof replacement represents a significant but highly strategic investment. The cost of a new roof varies based on the size of the home, the roofing material selected, the condition of the existing deck, and the complexity of the installation. In our experience across Broward County, most full roof replacements on a typical Lauderdale Lakes single-family home fall into a range that reflects the labor and materials needed to meet current Florida Building Code standards. CertainTeed Landmark shingle systems are generally the more accessible option, while West Lake tile systems carry a higher upfront cost but offer substantially longer service life and greater long-term ROI.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing understands that a new roof isn't always a planned expense. Storm damage, a failed inspection during a home sale, or a sudden leak can push this decision onto the table before a homeowner feels financially ready. That's why we offer flexible financing options starting as low as $87 per month, with $0 down programs available for qualified homeowners. Our financing is designed specifically with South Florida homeowners in mind — including those in lower-middle income communities like Lauderdale Lakes — because we believe that quality roofing should be accessible to every family, not just those who can pay out of pocket.

Beyond the financing conversation, it's worth considering the return on investment that a new roof delivers. A properly installed hurricane-rated roof in Lauderdale Lakes can meaningfully increase your home's appraisal value, improve its marketability when you're ready to sell, and dramatically reduce your exposure to out-of-pocket storm damage costs. Add in the insurance premium savings detailed in the next section, and the financial case for investing in a quality new roof becomes even more compelling.

section_insurance.h2: How a New Roof in Lauderdale Lakes Can Lower Your Homeowners Insurance Premiums
section_insurance.body:
Florida homeowners insurance is expensive — and in Broward County, it's among the priciest in the state. For Lauderdale Lakes homeowners, one of the most effective ways to reduce that ongoing cost is to invest in a new, code-compliant roof. Florida law allows for significant insurance discounts tied to wind mitigation features, and a new roof installed by a licensed contractor like Premier Impact Windows & Roofing comes with documentation that can unlock those credits immediately.

The key mechanism is the wind mitigation inspection, which is performed by a licensed inspector after your new roof is installed. This inspection evaluates features like roof deck attachment method, roof covering type, roof-to-wall connection strength, and roof shape — and the results are submitted to your insurance carrier. A new CertainTeed Landmark shingle or West Lake tile roof installed to current Florida Building Code standards will score well on these inspections, often resulting in annual premium reductions of hundreds of dollars. Over the life of your roof, those cumulative savings can offset a meaningful portion of your installation cost. Many Lauderdale Lakes homeowners are surprised to find that their new roof pays for itself faster than they expected once insurance credits are factored in.

It's also important to understand that insurance companies are increasingly scrutinizing older roofs and either refusing to renew policies on homes with aging roofs or requiring homeowners to carry coverage at higher rates. With 60% of Lauderdale Lakes homes built before 1994, a large share of the community's housing stock may be approaching — or already past — the age threshold that triggers these insurance challenges. A new roof from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing doesn't just protect your home from the next storm; it protects your ability to maintain affordable, comprehensive homeowners insurance coverage in a market that is becoming increasingly restrictive.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Lauderdale Lakes home needs a full roof replacement or just a repair?
faqs.a: The answer depends on several factors: the age of your roof, the extent and type of damage, and the condition of your roof deck. In Lauderdale Lakes, where about 60% of homes were built before 1994, many roofs are approaching or have already exceeded their intended service life. A professional inspection by Premier Impact Windows & Roofing will assess the overall system health and give you an honest recommendation — we'll recommend repair when it's truly sufficient, and replacement only when it's genuinely needed.

faqs.q: What does a new roof cost for a typical home in Lauderdale Lakes, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Roofing costs vary based on home size, materials selected, and deck condition, but Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing options starting as low as $87 per month with $0 down programs available for qualified homeowners. We provide fully transparent, itemized estimates so you know exactly what you're paying for before any work begins. Our goal is to make quality, hurricane-rated roofing accessible to every Lauderdale Lakes homeowner regardless of budget.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my roof in Lauderdale Lakes, and how does that process work?
faqs.a: Yes, all roof replacements in Lauderdale Lakes require a permit issued through the City of Lauderdale Lakes Building Division, followed by required inspections. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf — from application to final inspection sign-off. This protects you legally, keeps your homeowners insurance valid, and ensures your roof is installed to current Florida Building Code standards. We never recommend or perform unpermitted roofing work.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement typically take in Lauderdale Lakes?
faqs.a: Most full roof replacements on a standard Lauderdale Lakes single-family home are completed within one to three days, depending on the size of the home, the roofing material selected, and the condition of the existing deck. Tile installations may take slightly longer due to the precision required. We schedule work efficiently, keep the job site clean and organized throughout the process, and communicate proactively with homeowners so there are no surprises. Permit processing timelines can add a few days before work begins.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowners insurance in Broward County?
faqs.a: In many cases, yes — significantly. Florida law allows insurers to apply wind mitigation credits based on the features of your roof system, and a new hurricane-rated roof installed to current code will score well on a wind mitigation inspection. Many Lauderdale Lakes homeowners see annual premium reductions of several hundred dollars after installing a new CertainTeed or West Lake roof with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing. Over time, these savings can offset a meaningful portion of the installation cost. We recommend requesting a wind mitigation inspection promptly after your new roof is complete.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your Lauderdale Lakes Home with a Roof That's Built to Last?
cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for a free, no-pressure roof inspection and estimate — our team is ready to bring the Kings of Service standard to your Lauderdale Lakes home, with financing options that make it easier than ever to get started.
